target.title: fix(gateway): render codex inline media once
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Codex-harness replies that emitted local `MEDIA:` image directives could fail to become trusted outgoing media, leaving inline images text-only or unavailable to WebChat/Control UI.
- Why it matters: Agents using the Codex harness should be able to return generated/local images inline without requiring channel-specific manual workarounds, and users should not see duplicate assistant turns for one image reply.
- What changed: Trust structured local media markers from non-external tool results, while preserving the existing registered-tool trust checks; replace the latest raw assistant `MEDIA:` transcript turn with the normalized media-bearing message instead of appending a second assistant image turn.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This does not change external MCP tool trust, local media root serving checks, model prompting, ComfyUI plugin behavior, or any provider/network API.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

No matching upstream issue was found. Searches checked: `inline image`, `inline media`, `Codex harness image`, `MEDIA: image`, `assistant-media`, `managed media`, `Control UI image`, and duplicate image/WebChat terms.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: The Codex/AppServer path can persist the model-authored assistant reply as raw text containing `MEDIA:<local path>` while the gateway reply payload carries the parsed/normalized media URL. The WebChat transcript bridge treated those as separate assistant replies and appended the normalized media message after the raw directive turn.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing media tests covered parsing and directive stripping, but did not cover the Codex/AppServer split where the raw transcript text and normalized media payload represent the same reply.
- Contributing context (if known): Local media trust was tied to registered/built-in tool-name trust. Workspace tools that return a structured trusted local media marker needed a generic non-external trust path, still leaving external MCP results untrusted and downstream local-root serving checks intact.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - Non-external structured `trustedLocalMedia` tool results keep local media URLs, while external tool results with the same marker do not.
  - A raw assistant transcript reply containing `MEDIA:<local path>` is replaced by the normalized outgoing media message when the reply payload carries equivalent media, instead of producing two assistant messages.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These tests hit the two affected seams directly: tool-result media trust filtering and WebChat transcript persistence/replacement.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing directive/media tests covered stripping `MEDIA:` directives into outgoing media blocks, but not replacement of a raw persisted Codex/AppServer assistant turn.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A; new regression coverage is included.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Codex-harness agents can render trusted local image replies inline in WebChat/Control UI without leaving a raw `MEDIA:` line behind or showing a duplicate text-plus-image assistant reply.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[Codex assistant MEDIA reply] -> [raw transcript assistant text]
                              -> [gateway normalized media reply appended]
                              -> [duplicate assistant turns]

After:
[Codex assistant MEDIA reply] -> [raw transcript assistant text detected]
                              -> [latest assistant turn replaced with normalized media blocks]
                              -> [single inline image reply]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

Security notes:

- Structured local media trust is ignored for external MCP tool results.
- Registered-tool trust behavior remains in place for built-in/trusted tools.
- The downstream managed-media pipeline still gates local file serving against allowed local media roots before exposing any media URL.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux local development workspace; Windows gateway hot-patch was separately smoke-tested before PR preparation.
- Runtime/container: Node local workspace.
- Model/provider: Codex harness path observed with `gpt-5.5` during manual smoke testing.
- Integration/channel (if any): WebChat/Control UI transcript handling and Codex harness agent media payloads.
- Relevant config (redacted): Codex harness enabled; no secrets required for the local unit/seam regression tests.

### Steps

1. Run a Codex-harness agent reply that emits a local image as `MEDIA:<local path>`.
2. Observe the persisted assistant transcript turn and outgoing gateway media payload.
3. Open WebChat/Control UI for the same session.

### Expected

- The reply appears once as an inline image-bearing assistant message.
- The raw `MEDIA:` directive is not shown to the user.
- Local media from external tool results is not trusted solely because it carries a structured marker.

### Actual

- Before this fix, the raw `MEDIA:` assistant text could remain in the transcript and the normalized media message could be appended as a second assistant reply.
- Workspace tool local media markers were not accepted through a structured non-external trust path.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Passing local verification on the rebased branch:

```text
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
# passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
# passed

node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts
# 42 passed

OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PROJECT_SHARDS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts
# 132 passed
```

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - Rebased the fix onto current `openclaw/openclaw` `main`.
  - Verified the agent media trust tests pass.
  - Verified the gateway directive/WebChat media transcript tests pass.
  - Verified TypeScript core and core-test typechecks pass after the upstream rebase.
  - Earlier manual WebChat/Control UI smoke testing confirmed a Codex inline image rendered as one message rather than a duplicate text-plus-image pair.
- Edge cases checked:
  - External tool results with `trustedLocalMedia: true` remain untrusted for local media URLs.
  - Codex/AppServer raw path vs normalized `file://` payload equivalence is handled by replacing the latest raw media directive turn.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - Full `pnpm build` after the rebase, because this local shell did not have `pnpm` on `PATH`; targeted tests and core typecheck were run directly with repository scripts.
  - Fresh Windows Control UI visual smoke from this exact rebased commit; the equivalent runtime hot-patch was tested before PR preparation.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

N/A for initial PR submission.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Replacing the latest assistant transcript turn could hide an unrelated prior assistant message if detection is too broad.
  - Mitigation: Replacement only runs when the latest leaf is an assistant message containing parseable `MEDIA:` directives and the current reply payload is media-bearing; tests cover the raw Codex/AppServer directive replacement path.
- Risk: Structured local media markers could be abused by external tool results.
  - Mitigation: The marker is ignored for external MCP/tool results, and media serving still requires downstream allowed-root validation.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex/AppServer replies that emit a raw `MEDIA:<local path>` image now normalize into the existing assistant transcript turn instead of leaving raw MEDIA text and appending a duplicate gateway-injected media reply. Current head tested: 2ce02670381be9035fc3cb98ae89118d37b29482.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw PR checkout on Linux, Node v24.13.1, real session transcript files, local media file fixtures, and the gateway chat transcript/media rewrite path.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: From the PR checkout after applying the ClawSweeper review fixes, ran `git diff --check` and `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 300s pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
$ PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 300s pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts
$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.6 /tmp/openclaw-pr-resolve-20260517

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  77 passed (77)
   Start at  00:34:46
   Duration  12.92s (transform 2.53s, setup 291ms, import 6.75s, tests 5.52s, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 24.78s
```

- Observed result after fix: The focused gateway transcript/media suite passed on current head, including coverage that preserves captions while stripping raw `MEDIA:` directives, refuses to rewrite an older matching media turn after a newer assistant turn, and avoids a second message-level transcript update after the file-level rewrite event.
- What was not tested: I did not run a fresh browser visual smoke for this exact head; the current-head proof covers the real transcript/media rewrite behavior that ClawSweeper flagged.

target.body:
## Summary

target.title: fix(agents): reserve 25% context window for input tokens in maxTokens calculation
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** When OpenClaw sends API requests with large-context models (e.g., StepFun step-router-v1 with 262144 context), `maxTokens` was set to the full `contextWindow` without subtracting already-used input tokens. This caused API "max_tokens is too large" errors.
- **What changed:** Reserve 25% of context window for input tokens by setting `maxTokens = Math.floor(contextWindow * 0.75)`
- **Why it matters:** Prevents API rejection on large-context models while maintaining reasonable output token limits.

## Root Cause

**Location:** `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.ts` Lines 659-662

**Original code:**
```typescript
maxTokens:
  typeof resolvedContextWindow === "number"
    ? Math.min(resolvedMaxTokens, resolvedContextWindow)
    : resolvedMaxTokens,
```

**Problem:** No consideration for already-consumed input tokens.

## Fix

**New code:**
```typescript
maxTokens:
  typeof resolvedContextWindow === "number"
    ? Math.min(resolvedMaxTokens, Math.floor(resolvedContextWindow * 0.75))
    : resolvedMaxTokens,
```

**Logic:** Reserve 25% of context window for input tokens.

## Testing

- **Test case:** Large-context model (262144 tokens) with 100k input tokens
- **Before fix:** API rejects with "max_tokens is too large"
- **After fix:** API accepts request (maxTokens=196608)

## Security Impact

1. **Does this change auth/authentication logic?** No
2. **Does this change audit logging?** No
3. **Does this change session key/account isolation?** No
4. **Does this change message routing/visibility?** No
5. **Does this introduce new external API calls?** No

## Changelog

✅ Updated CHANGELOG.md

Fixes #83086

---

target.title: fix(gateway): report unqueued restart continuations
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `gateway` restart with `continuationMessage` could return success while coalescing with an already in-flight restart, even though the continuation hook was never queued.
- Why it matters: the agent/user saw a successful restart response but the promised follow-up turn was silently lost.
- What changed: `scheduleGatewaySigusr1Restart` now reports whether emit hooks were queued, and the gateway tool reports `continuationQueued` when a continuation was requested.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this PR does not attempt to merge a new continuation into an already emitted restart; it makes the non-delivery explicit so callers can react.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #74424
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: in-flight restart coalescing reports that continuation hooks were not queued.
- Real environment tested: macOS 26.5, Node v22.22.0, pnpm 11.1.0, source checkout at `upstream/main` plus this one commit.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
node --import tsx -e 'import { __testing, emitGatewayRestart, scheduleGatewaySigusr1Restart } from "./src/infra/restart.ts"; __testing.resetSigusr1State(); let beforeEmitCalls=0; const handler=()=>{}; process.on("SIGUSR1", handler); try { const emitted=emitGatewayRestart("manual-proof"); const result=scheduleGatewaySigusr1Restart({delayMs:0,reason:"manual-proof-2",emitHooks:{beforeEmit:async()=>{ beforeEmitCalls+=1; }}}); console.log(JSON.stringify({emitted,coalesced:result.coalesced,emitHooksQueued:result.emitHooksQueued,beforeEmitCalls,continuationQueuedWouldBe:result.emitHooksQueued}, null, 2)); } finally { process.removeListener("SIGUSR1", handler); __testing.resetSigusr1State(); }'
```

- Evidence after fix (terminal capture):

```text
[restart] request coalesced (already in-flight) reason=manual-proof-2 actor=<unknown>
{
  "emitted": true,
  "coalesced": true,
  "emitHooksQueued": false,
  "beforeEmitCalls": 0,
  "continuationQueuedWouldBe": false
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the scheduler reports `emitHooksQueued: false` in the already-emitted coalescing path, so the gateway tool can report `continuationQueued: false`.
- What was not tested: a full live gateway restart cycle with a real user-visible post-restart continuation; the in-process proof exercises the exact scheduler branch that previously accepted hooks without running or retaining them.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): issue #74424 includes the observed successful/coalesced restart response with no follow-up turn.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `scheduleGatewaySigusr1Restart` returned early for `hasUnconsumedRestartSignal()` before registering or running the caller's `emitHooks`, while the gateway tool returned the scheduler success shape without saying the continuation was not queued.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no regression covered the already-emitted coalescing branch with restart continuation hooks.
- Contributing context (if known): config/plugin workflows can schedule restarts while an agent also calls `gateway.restart` with `continuationMessage`.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/infra/infra-runtime.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/gateway-tool.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - scheduled coalescing with hooks reports `emitHooksQueued: true`
  - already-emitted coalescing with hooks reports `emitHooksQueued: false`
  - gateway restart result includes `continuationQueued: false` and does not write a sentinel when hooks were not queued
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the dropped continuation happened in the restart scheduler/tool seam, before any post-restart delivery code could run.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none before this PR.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`gateway.restart` results with a requested continuation can now include `continuationQueued`. In already in-flight coalescing cases this is `false` instead of silently implying the continuation was queued.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
restart already emitted -> gateway restart with continuation -> coalesced ok -> continuation silently lost

After:
restart already emitted -> gateway restart with continuation -> coalesced ok + continuationQueued=false
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 26.5
- Runtime/container: Node v22.22.0, pnpm 11.1.0
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): gateway restart scheduler/tool seam
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Emit an in-process gateway restart signal with a SIGUSR1 handler installed.
2. Schedule a second restart with `emitHooks.beforeEmit`.
3. Inspect the scheduler result and hook call count.

### Expected

- The second request coalesces.
- The scheduler reports hooks were not queued.
- `beforeEmit` is not called.

### Actual

- Matches expected after this PR.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Screenshots are not attached because this is backend restart-scheduler behavior; copied terminal captures are included above.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:

target.title: fix(codex): keep owner-only tool schema stable
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Codex app-server dynamic tool schema changed when a turn was temporarily downgraded from owner to non-owner, causing native thread churn.
- Why it matters: long-lived Codex sessions could lose native thread continuity and repeat startup/bootstrap work when owner-only tool availability flipped.
- What changed: Codex app-server now keeps owner-only tool schemas visible for stable thread-level schema identity while OpenClaw still guards execution fail-closed for unauthorized turns.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): default non-Codex tool filtering remains unchanged; unauthorized owner-only tool calls still fail.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature

- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #76179
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: retain owner-only schemas for a downgraded runtime turn while preserving the execution guard.
- Real environment tested: macOS 26.5, Node v22.22.0, pnpm 11.1.0, source checkout at `upstream/main` plus this one commit.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
node --import tsx -e 'import { applyOwnerOnlyToolPolicy } from "./src/agents/tool-policy.ts"; const tools=[{name:"read"},{name:"cron",ownerOnly:true,execute:async()=>"ran"},{name:"gateway",ownerOnly:true,execute:async()=>"ran"}]; const defaultNames=applyOwnerOnlyToolPolicy(tools,false).map((tool)=>tool.name); const stable=applyOwnerOnlyToolPolicy(tools,false,undefined,{retainUnauthorizedOwnerOnlyTools:true}); let guard="not-run"; try { await stable.find((tool)=>tool.name==="cron")?.execute?.("manual-proof", {}); } catch (err) { guard=String(err instanceof Error ? err.message : err); } console.log(JSON.stringify({defaultNames,stableNames:stable.map((tool)=>tool.name),guard}, null, 2));'
```

- Evidence after fix (terminal capture):

```json
{
  "defaultNames": [
    "read"
  ],
  "stableNames": [
    "read",
    "cron",
    "gateway"
  ],
  "guard": "Tool restricted to owner senders."
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the stable-schema option retains owner-only tool names, and unauthorized execution still throws the owner-only guard error.
- What was not tested: live Feishu DM traffic and live Codex native thread IDs; the PR includes deterministic tests for policy retention, `createOpenClawCodingTools`, and Codex app-server option propagation.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): issue #76179 includes trajectory rows showing tool count flip `71 <-> 74` and native thread restarts.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `applyOwnerOnlyToolPolicy` removed owner-only tools from non-owner turns, and Codex app-server included that per-turn catalog in native thread schema identity.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no test asserted that Codex app-server can request a stable schema while execution remains guarded.
- Contributing context (if known): temporary ownership downgrades can happen while draining untrusted system events.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/agents/tool-policy.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-tools.owner-only-gating.test.ts`
  - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - unauthorized owner-only tools can be retained for schema stability
  - retained tools still throw `Tool restricted to owner senders.`
  - Codex dynamic tool construction opts into this stable-schema mode
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug was a dynamic-tool schema identity issue, not a live channel transport issue.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none before this PR.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Codex app-server sessions should avoid native thread churn when the only tool-catalog difference is temporary owner-only authorization. Unauthorized owner-only calls still fail.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
owner turn -> 74 tools -> Codex thread A
downgraded turn -> 71 tools -> schema mismatch -> Codex thread B

After:
owner turn -> stable schemas
downgraded turn -> stable schemas + execution guard -> same native thread can continue
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Codex receives stable owner-only tool schemas for this runtime path, but unauthorized execution is wrapped before the tool implementation and fails with `Tool restricted to owner senders.`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 26.5
- Runtime/container: Node v22.22.0, pnpm 11.1.0
- Model/provider: Codex app-server path; no live provider call used
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A for local proof; issue was observed in Feishu DM
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

2. Compare default filtering with the stable-schema option.

  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tool-policy.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.owner-only-gating.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/schema-normalization-runtime-contract.test.ts` passed: 6 files, 241 tests.
  - `node scripts/check-changed.mjs --base upstream/main` passed: `core`, `coreTests`, `extensions`, `extensionTests`.
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 ...` passed for all 6 touched files.
  - `git diff --check` passed.
- Edge cases checked:
  - default non-owner filtering still removes owner-only tools
  - stable-schema mode keeps owner-only tools visible
  - retained owner-only execution remains guarded
- What you did **not** verify:

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.

- Risk: making owner-only schemas visible to Codex on downgraded turns could look like broadened access.

target.title: fix(gateway): preserve raw external session aliases
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: raw structured external session keys such as `conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123` could be pruned as legacy aliases after gateway agent canonicalization.
- Why it matters: the next inbound message for the same raw external key could miss the existing session entry and create a fresh `sessionId`, splitting one logical conversation.
- What changed: `migrateAndPruneGatewaySessionStoreKey` now preserves non-agent raw structured external aliases and mirrors the canonical entry back to that raw key.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not change agent-scoped key canonicalization, reset/rollover behavior, or migrate all historical store keys.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #63005
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: raw external key remains usable after canonical gateway session migration/pruning.
- Real environment tested: macOS 26.5, Node v22.22.0, pnpm 11.1.0, source checkout at `upstream/main` plus this one commit.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
node --import tsx -e 'import { migrateAndPruneGatewaySessionStoreKey } from "./src/gateway/session-utils.ts"; const raw="conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123"; const cfg={session:{mainKey:"main"},agents:{list:[{id:"main",default:true}]}}; const store={ [raw]: { sessionId:"sess-raw", updatedAt:2 } }; const result=migrateAndPruneGatewaySessionStoreKey({cfg,key:raw,store}); console.log(JSON.stringify({primaryKey:result.primaryKey,resultSessionId:result.entry?.sessionId,rawSessionId:store[raw]?.sessionId,canonicalSessionId:store[result.primaryKey]?.sessionId,keys:Object.keys(store).sort()}, null, 2));'
```

- Evidence after fix (terminal capture):

```json
{
  "primaryKey": "agent:main:conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123",
  "resultSessionId": "sess-raw",
  "rawSessionId": "sess-raw",
  "canonicalSessionId": "sess-raw",
  "keys": [
    "agent:main:conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123",
    "conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123"
  ]
}
```

- Observed result after fix: both the canonical key and raw external key point at the same `sessionId`.
- What was not tested: a live third-party external integration; this uses the sanitized key shape from the issue against the real session-store migration helper.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): issue #63005 documents the old split shape and the pruned raw-key path.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the gateway canonicalized non-agent session keys to `agent:<default>:<raw>`, then treated the original raw structured external key as a legacy candidate during pruning.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no regression asserted that a structured external key survives canonical writeback for later inbound raw-key lookup.
- Contributing context (if known): inbound/session-init paths may still receive and look up the raw external key.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: migrating `conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123` preserves both raw and canonical store keys with the same session entry.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the split came from alias pruning in the session-store helper, so the deterministic helper test catches it without channel credentials.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none before this PR.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Existing external conversations that rely on stable structured raw keys are less likely to split after internal gateway/nested-agent activity.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
raw external key -> canonical gateway key -> raw alias pruned -> later raw inbound can start a new session

After:
raw external key -> canonical gateway key -> raw alias preserved -> later raw inbound resolves the same session
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 26.5
- Runtime/container: Node v22.22.0, pnpm 11.1.0
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): sanitized external session key shape from #63005
- Relevant config (redacted): default `main` agent config shape in the regression

### Steps

1. Seed a session store with raw key `conversation:pair:user_a::user_b:space:space_123`.
2. Call `migrateAndPruneGatewaySessionStoreKey` for that key.
3. Inspect the store keys and `sessionId`.

### Expected

- The canonical key exists and uses the existing raw session entry.
- The raw external key remains present for future raw inbound lookups.

### Actual

- Matches expected after this PR.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

  - `node --import tsx -e ...` manual probe above.
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts` passed: 3 files, 264 tests.
  - `node scripts/check-changed.mjs --base upstream/main` passed: `core`, `coreTests`.
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/session-utils.ts src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts` passed.
  - `git diff --check` passed.
- Edge cases checked:
  - raw key contains structured `:` delimiters
  - canonical key and raw alias both reuse `sess-raw`
- What you did **not** verify:
  - live third-party external integration traffic

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

target.title: Fix Codex runtime hook and session churn
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes two Codex-runtime stability problems reported by users:

- Fixes #76552: Codex no longer installs the default `PreToolUse` native hook relay command when no `before_tool_call` policy exists, avoiding one `openclaw hooks relay ... --event pre_tool_use` child process per native Codex tool call.
- Fixes #76179: Codex keeps owner-only dynamic tool schemas stable across sender-owner flips, so app-server threads resume instead of constantly starting fresh native Codex sessions.

## Root Cause

For #76552, current `main` already avoided relay subprocesses for `PostToolUse` and finalization hooks when no local handlers exist, but `pre_tool_use` still returned `true` unconditionally in `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts`. That meant default Codex app-server config could still register a `PreToolUse` command hook that shells out through the OpenClaw relay for every native tool use, even when there was no OpenClaw `before_tool_call` policy to run.

For #76179, the Codex app-server thread fingerprint included the dynamic tool registration set. Owner-only tools were filtered out for non-owner senders, so an owner/non-owner context change could alter the registered dynamic tool names and force a `thread/start` instead of `thread/resume`. Since Codex receives dynamic tool schemas on thread start, not as a cheap per-turn update, this made a transient availability flip expensive and session-churning.

## What Changed

- `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts` now only reports local `pre_tool_use` relay work when `hasBeforeToolCallPolicy()` is true. `permission_request` remains active because Codex approvals still need the app-server bridge.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts` opts Codex dynamic tool construction into stable owner-only schema registration.
- `src/agents/tool-policy.ts` can retain unavailable owner-only tools while wrapping their execution with a fail-closed authorization guard.
- `src/agents/pi-tools.ts` passes the new retain option through to the owner-only policy layer.

## Before / After

Before:

- Default Codex sessions could register a `PreToolUse` relay command even with no `before_tool_call` plugin policy, so multiple native tools could fan out into multiple OpenClaw relay CLI child processes.
- Switching from an owner sender to a non-owner sender could change dynamic tool registration and force a new Codex native thread.

After:

- No `PreToolUse` relay command is installed unless there is actual before-tool policy work.
- Command approvals still get the in-process `nativeHookRelay` bridge for `pre_tool_use` decisions.
- Codex dynamic tool schemas stay stable across owner-only availability flips, but unauthorized owner-only tool execution still throws `Tool restricted to owner senders.`
- Owner to non-owner turns reuse the same Codex thread via `thread/resume`.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex runtime process/session churn. Specifically: default app-server runs should not install a `PreToolUse` relay command when no `before_tool_call` policy exists, and owner-only dynamic tool availability changes should not force a fresh Codex native thread while unauthorized owner-only execution still fails closed.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS OpenClaw checkout at PR head `484cf48b97`, Node `v22.22.0`, Codex CLI logged in with ChatGPT auth, OpenClaw bundled `codex` plugin loaded from this branch. Private home/workspace paths and auth details redacted below.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran two real local Codex app-server OpenClaw turns on the same session, then inspected the redacted trajectory/sidecar, and ran a throwaway production-code runtime probe with `node --import tsx` to print the hook config, owner-only tool policy result, and thread lifecycle request sequence.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text

Logged in using ChatGPT

$ OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug pnpm openclaw agent --local --model openai/gpt-5.4 --session-id codex-runtime-proof-83367 --message "Reply with exactly: OK" --json --timeout 180

$ jq '{threadId, model, modelProvider, createdAt, updatedAt, dynamicToolsFingerprintPresent:(.dynamicToolsFingerprint!=null), sessionFile:"<redacted-session-file>"}' <redacted-session>.jsonl.codex-app-server.json

```

Production-code runtime probe output:

```json
{
  "hookRelay": {
    "preToolUse": [],
    "postToolUse": [],
    "stop": [],
    "permissionRequestCommandIncludes": {
      "permission_request": true,
      "pre_tool_use": false
    }
  },

      "cron",

  },

    ],

    ],

      [
        "cron",

      [],
      []
    ]
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The real Codex app-server OpenClaw session completed two live `openai-codex/gpt-5.4` turns with the same native thread id. The runtime probe shows default hook config sends `hooks.PreToolUse: []` while keeping `PermissionRequest` relay active, retained owner-only tool names are stable for owner and non-owner policy inputs, unauthorized owner-only execution throws `Tool restricted to owner senders.`, and lifecycle calls are `thread/start` followed by `thread/resume`.
- What was not tested: I did not run a live external chat-channel ingress scenario that naturally flips `senderIsOwner`; that path needs real channel identity setup. The same owner/non-owner availability flip was exercised through the production owner-only policy and Codex thread lifecycle code path in the runtime probe above, and the local live Codex session verified the actual app-server harness/auth path.

## Tests / Proof

No product UI screenshot is applicable here because the bugs are process/session churn in the Codex runtime path. I included the terminal proof below so reviewers can see exactly what was exercised.

Targeted formatter and whitespace checks:

```text

target.title: fix(outbound): log failDelivery rejection on bestEffort partial failure (#83113)
target.body:
Closes #83113

## Problem

In `src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts` (around line 1298), when best-effort partial delivery occurs the queue entry is marked failed via `failDelivery()` so the retry count is incremented. The previous code wrapped that call in `.catch(() => {})`, silently swallowing any rejection from `failDelivery` itself.

If `failDelivery` rejects (e.g. queue storage write fails), the entry's retry count is never incremented, recovery may retry the un-acked entry, and the operator gets no journal-facing hint that anything went wrong. This is inconsistent with the surrounding `ackDelivery` path (~lines 1305-1316) which logs warnings via `log.warn` + `formatErrorMessage`.

## Fix

Replace the silent `.catch(() => {})` with a `log.warn` that includes:

- the queue id
- a hint that the queue entry may be retried
- `formatErrorMessage(err)` for the underlying cause

This matches the existing `ackDelivery` warning style. The non-required best-effort branch keeps continuing (no rethrow) so behavior on success paths is unchanged — only the silent-failure observability gap is closed.

## Test

Added regression test `logs a warning when failDelivery itself rejects on bestEffort partial failure` in `src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts` that mocks `failDelivery` to reject and asserts the warn line contains the queue id and underlying error message.

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts
# 83 tests passed (1 new)
```

Lint clean (`oxlint`), no other call sites touched. Narrowly scoped to the specifically-flagged path; the other `failDelivery(...).catch(() => {})` on line ~1328 is a different control flow (the outer catch already has a logged error to work from) and is intentionally left untouched to keep the PR small.

## Found by

Codebase audit (finding F019), reported in #83113.

target.title: fix(scripts): close local heavy-check lock gaps
target.body:
## Summary
- keep the shared local heavy-check lock for type-aware oxlint, including explicit file-target runs that can spawn `tsgolint headless`
- wrap sharded oxlint in one parent heavy-check lock while child shards skip duplicate locks to avoid self-deadlock
- stop exempting scoped tooling test runs from the shared heavy-check lock
- add a source-checkout install pressure guard so local `pnpm install` refuses to start when memory/swap/load are already dangerous
- clean up two lint-only failures on the branch: a dead Codex test helper and the cron list pagination loop shape

## Why
A Pi incident showed `tsgolint` entering through type-aware oxlint and consuming ~5.25 GiB RSS while swap was nearly exhausted. Direct `run-tsgo` was guarded, but type-aware oxlint and scoped tooling tests could still bypass the shared lock and run beside other heavy local work.

The later post-incident sweep found one more practical pressure path: local source-checkout installs could start while the host was already choking. This PR now blocks that path unless explicitly overridden.

## Behavior
- Local heavy-check serialization now covers:
  - type-aware oxlint with explicit file targets
  - sharded oxlint parent runs
  - scoped tooling test runs
- Shard children skip duplicate locking after the parent lock is acquired.
- Local source-checkout installs refuse under host pressure when any of these are true:
  - `MemAvailable < 2GiB`
  - `SwapFree < 1GiB`
  - `load1 > 10`
- CI/GitHub Actions skip the install refusal.
- Local install refusal can be bypassed with `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_PRESSURE_GUARD=0`.

## Test Plan
- `node --check scripts/lib/local-heavy-check-runtime.mjs`
- `node --check scripts/test-projects.test-support.mjs`
- `node --check scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs`
- `node --check scripts/preinstall-package-manager-warning.mjs`
- `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_FORCE_LOCK=1 OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled pnpm test test/scripts/local-heavy-check-runtime.test.ts test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts test/scripts/run-oxlint.test.ts test/scripts/preinstall-package-manager-warning.test.ts --run`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 scripts/lib/local-heavy-check-runtime.mjs scripts/test-projects.test-support.mjs scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs scripts/preinstall-package-manager-warning.mjs test/scripts/local-heavy-check-runtime.test.ts test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts test/scripts/run-oxlint.test.ts test/scripts/preinstall-package-manager-warning.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/tools/cron-tool.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts --threads=1`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/tools/cron-tool.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled pnpm test src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.schema.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.flat-params.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts --run`
- `git diff --check`




## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: The local heavy-check guards now keep type-aware oxlint and sharded oxlint behind the shared local heavy-check lock, refuse pressured local installs, and avoid duplicate child-shard locking while still allowing child shards to run under the parent lock.

Real environment tested: Real OpenClaw source checkout on Raspberry Pi host, rebased onto current `origin/main` at the time of repair. The host was under real pressure during verification (`SwapFree below 1GiB`, `load1 above 10`), which exercised the install pressure guard path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Rebased the PR branch onto current `origin/main`.
2. Ran `git merge-base --is-ancestor origin/main HEAD`.
3. Ran `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`.
4. Ran `pnpm test test/scripts/local-heavy-check-runtime.test.ts test/scripts/preinstall-package-manager-warning.test.ts test/scripts/run-oxlint.test.ts test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts`, which hit the real install pressure guard.
5. Ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/local-heavy-check-runtime.test.ts test/scripts/preinstall-package-manager-warning.test.ts test/scripts/run-oxlint.test.ts test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts` to verify the targeted behavior without dependency reconciliation in the pressured worktree.

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the real Raspberry Pi checkout showed the normal `pnpm test` wrapper refusing unsafe local work with `Refusing to run package-manager install/update on this host because SwapFree below 1GiB and load1 above 10`. The same terminal session then showed targeted `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ...` output passing 4 files / 127 tests.

Observed result after fix: The runtime guard refused pressured local dependency reconciliation, while the targeted Vitest command passed the affected local-heavy-check behavior without bypassing the guard. This proves the lock/refusal behavior on the actual local host rather than only in CI.

What was not tested: I did not run broad/full local heavy checks on the Raspberry Pi because this change intentionally prevents unsafe heavy local work under pressure. CI remains the broad proof surface for the full matrix.

target.title: feat(telegram): bind spawned subagents to forum topics
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds an explicit `supportsAutomaticThreadBindingSpawn` channel capability so automatic thread-bound session spawn support is not overloaded onto `defaultTopLevelPlacement`.
- Wires Telegram `subagent_spawning`, `subagent_delivery_target`, and `subagent_ended` hooks so `sessions_spawn(thread:true)` for subagents can create and bind a child forum topic while keeping Telegram's normal top-level binding placement as `current`.
- Makes the spawn capability per-kind (`subagent` vs `acp`) so Telegram advertises subagent forum-topic spawn support without incorrectly exposing ACP child-topic spawn.
- Documents the new capability seam and adds Telegram/core regression coverage for schema exposure, runtime capabilities, hook registration, binding, completion routing, cleanup, and boundary invariants.

## Context

Telegram already had forum-topic primitives and session binding infrastructure, but it lacked the subagent lifecycle hook wiring and an explicit capability to say: "explicit subagent thread spawns can create child topics, while ordinary top-level binding should stay in the current conversation." Discord and Matrix naturally expose automatic thread spawns through `defaultTopLevelPlacement: "child"`; Feishu keeps `current` placement and does not advertise child-thread session spawn. Telegram now follows the narrower model: keep normal Telegram `/focus` and top-level binding behavior current, while allowing explicit `sessions_spawn(thread:true)` for subagents to create a child forum topic when the Telegram adapter can do so.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram `sessions_spawn(thread:true, mode:"session")` from a channel-bound CodeClaw triage session creates and binds a real Telegram forum topic for the spawned subagent session, so follow-up work routes to that topic instead of becoming invisible or falling back to the parent topic.
- Real environment tested: Live `codeclaw-triage` Docker container after installing a custom OpenClaw package built from combined proof branch `codeclaw-proof-82023-82275` (https://github.com/efpiva/openclaw/tree/codeclaw-proof-82023-82275) at `OpenClaw 2026.5.17 (db1c53a)`. The container was connected to the real CodeClaw Telegram forum supergroup and used the real CodeClaw triage cron job.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```bash
  docker exec --user codeclaw codeclaw-triage     HOME=/home/codeclaw openclaw cron run cc373f35-b483-4c7f-9253-2f6c9c94a595       --wait --wait-timeout 15m --timeout 30000
  ```

- Evidence after fix:

  The triage transcript for run `1d6d77dd-c23b-4b02-9831-e990fb11a7fc` shows the initial PR action for private CodeClaw PR #1223 was spawned with `thread:true` and `mode:"session"`:

  ```json
  {
    "type": "toolCall",
    "name": "sessions_spawn",
    "arguments": {
      "agentId": "main",
      "label": "codeclaw-pr:external:microsoft.ghe.com:bic/lobster:1223",
      "mode": "session",
      "context": "isolated",
      "thread": true
    }
  }
  ```

  The tool result accepted the spawn and returned a thread-bound child session:

  ```json
  {
    "toolName": "sessions_spawn",
    "isError": false,
    "details": {
      "status": "accepted",
      "childSessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:c89515e3-1a1e-4465-8fe9-8b25e85c7819",
      "runId": "23538111-3540-4645-a66b-cc0568f96de0",
      "mode": "session",
      "note": "thread-bound session stays active after this task; continue in-thread for follow-ups.",
      "modelApplied": true
    }
  }
  ```

  Live Telegram/OpenClaw logs show the forum topic was created and bound to that child session. The Telegram supergroup id is redacted, but the topic id and session key are unchanged:

  ```text
  2026-05-17T15:07:57.282-07:00 telegram: bound conversation <telegram-supergroup-id>:topic:1167 -> agent:main:subagent:c89515e3-1a1e-4465-8fe9-8b25e85c7819 (idle=24h maxAge=disabled)
  2026-05-17T15:07:57.406-07:00 [telegram] update: {"message":{"message_id":1167,"chat":{"title":"CodeClaw Activity","is_forum":true,"type":"supergroup"},"message_thread_id":1167,"forum_topic_created":{"name":"🤖 codeclaw-pr:external:microsoft.ghe.com:bic/lobster:1223"},"is_topic_message":true}}
  2026-05-17T15:07:57.923-07:00 [agent/embedded] embedded run start: runId=23538111-3540-4645-a66b-cc0568f96de0 sessionId=ada5b4d0-65b5-48ff-8c81-76c2ab477ab8 provider=github-copilot model=gpt-5.5 thinking=medium messageChannel=telegram
  2026-05-17T15:07:58.444-07:00 [agent/embedded] [context-diag] pre-prompt: sessionKey=agent:main:subagent:c89515e3-1a1e-4465-8fe9-8b25e85c7819 ... sessionFile=/home/codeclaw/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/ada5b4d0-65b5-48ff-8c81-76c2ab477ab8-topic-1167.jsonl
  ```

  The persisted Telegram binding file contains the same mapping:

  ```json
  {
    "conversationId": "<telegram-supergroup-id>:topic:1167",
    "targetKind": "subagent",
    "targetSessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:c89515e3-1a1e-4465-8fe9-8b25e85c7819",
    "agentId": "main",
    "label": "codeclaw-pr:external:microsoft.ghe.com:bic/lobster:1223",
    "metadata": {
      "threadName": "🤖 codeclaw-pr:external:microsoft.ghe.com:bic/lobster:1223"
    }
  }
  ```

  The CodeClaw poller state also persisted the spawned native session key for that PR:

  ```json
  {
    "source": "pr_reviews",
    "id": "microsoft.ghe.com|bic/lobster#1223",
    "lastKind": "initial",
    "nativeSessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:c89515e3-1a1e-4465-8fe9-8b25e85c7819"
  }
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The live Telegram Bot API created forum topic `1167`, OpenClaw bound it to the spawned `main` subagent session, the child session ran with `messageChannel=telegram`, and all child/specialist session transcript files for the spawned review used the `topic-1167` suffix. CodeClaw recorded `nativeSessionKey` for future follow-ups.
- What was not tested: Telegram permission-denied and non-forum failure paths in the live container; those remain covered by local mocked tests. The live proof used one real forum supergroup and one real CodeClaw triage run.

## Supplemental validation


Behavior addressed: Telegram subagent `sessions_spawn(thread:true)` now has an explicit per-kind automatic-spawn capability and lifecycle hooks that bind spawned subagents to child forum topics without enabling Telegram ACP child-topic spawns.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout with mocked Telegram forum topic creation and session binding adapters; no live Telegram Bot API/forum supergroup was used.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  extensions/telegram/src/subagent-hooks.test.ts \
  extensions/telegram/index.test.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/thread-bindings.test.ts \
  src/channels/plugins/thread-binding-api.test.ts \
  src/channels/thread-bindings-policy.test.ts \
  src/agents/runtime-capabilities.test.ts \
  src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts \
  src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/boundary-invariants.test.ts
pnpm docs:check-mdx docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md
git diff --check
pnpm build
codex review --uncommitted
```

Evidence after fix: Targeted Vitest proof passed with 12 files and 245 tests; docs MDX check passed; whitespace check passed; `pnpm build` completed including plugin SDK export verification; final Codex CLI review reported no actionable correctness issues.

Observed result after fix: Telegram advertises `thread` for subagent session spawn through the new explicit per-kind capability, returns a routable Telegram delivery origin for created forum topics, routes completion to the bound topic, cleans up Telegram subagent bindings, and does not advertise/recommend ACP thread-bound spawns when ACP support is disabled for Telegram.

What was not tested: Live Telegram Bot API behavior in a real forum supergroup with `can_manage_topics`; non-forum group and permission failure behavior were covered only through local unit paths/mocked failures.

target.title: Route allow-always through command authorization planner
target.body:
## Summary
- Add the command authorization planner contract and Tree-sitter-backed POSIX planner corpus.
- Route POSIX allowlist evaluation, allow-always persistence, approval display summaries, control-command rejection, and enforced shell rendering through the planner.
- Remove the legacy POSIX chain/pipeline/heredoc parser from exec approval analysis; the remaining shell analyzer path is Windows-only.
- Preserve first-miss chain behavior, safe wrapper payload handling, positional carrier guards, pipe-to-shell prompting, and strict inline-eval no-persistence behavior.

## Real Behavior Proof
- Ran a live terminal proof against this branch, outside the unit harness.
- Command: `fnm exec --using 22.21.0 pnpm exec tsx -e '<planner allowlist proof>'`
- Result: planner-rendered enforced pipeline `'/usr/bin/printf' 'hi' | '/usr/bin/wc' '-c'` executed through `/bin/sh -lc` and returned `2`.
- Result: allow-always on `sh -c 'echo ok'` persisted `/bin/echo`; a later `sh -c 'id > /tmp/openclaw-parser-proof-marker'` still had `allowlistSatisfied: false`.

## Verification
- `fnm exec --using 22.21.0 pnpm test src/infra/command-authorization/corpus.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-analysis.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-allow-always.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-store.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-safe-bins.test.ts src/infra/command-analysis/explain.test.ts src/infra/command-analysis/explain.lazy.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-parity.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.test.ts src/node-host/invoke-system-run.test.ts`

target.title: fix(auth): drop stale Codex OAuth routing
target.body:
## Summary

- expand OAuth refresh-failure classification to match current Codex refresh-token reuse, expiry, invalidation, revocation, and account-mismatch messages
- remove the legacy Codex OAuth sidecar/Keychain path and keep auth-profile persistence on the OpenClaw credentials boundary
- keep stale persisted/session-selected Codex auth profiles and stale `auth.order.openai` entries from switching OpenAI PI runs to `openai-codex`
- skip Codex harness auth bootstrap when there is no eligible forwarded plugin harness profile
- add changelog coverage for the Codex auth behavior and fold the #83345 fix into this PR

Fixes #83223.
Supersedes #83345.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-refresh-failure.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/auth-bridge.test.ts src/commands/doctor-auth.hints.test.ts src/commands/status-all/gateway.test.ts src/commands/doctor-auth-oauth-sidecar.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/persisted-boundary.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/agents/openai-codex-routing.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.e2e.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts`: passed, 12 files / 195 tests
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-codex-routing.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.e2e.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts`: passed, 3 files / 67 tests
- `git diff --check`: passed
- Testbox-through-Crabbox `check:changed`: passed on `tbx_01krwna67n49v7tsqfs4j59ke7`, Actions run `https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26013454900`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex app-server OAuth refresh failures now classify current Codex terminal refresh-token states precisely while user-facing messages explain that OpenClaw cannot use the user's OpenAI account for Codex until they sign in again. Generic account-state drift remains retryable/non-terminal. Stale Codex profile references from prior config/session state no longer force OpenAI PI runs onto the `openai-codex` provider when the requested route is `openai/gpt-5.5`.

Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox via Crabbox wrapper using the OpenClaw CI check workflow.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run -provider blacksmith-testbox -blacksmith-workflow ci-check-testbox.yml -blacksmith-job check -keep-on-failure --shell -- "git fetch origin main && pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false check:changed"`.

Evidence after fix: focused regression suites passed locally in the Codex worktree, and `check:changed` completed successfully on Testbox `tbx_01krwna67n49v7tsqfs4j59ke7` for commit `9bc371033cc`.

target.title: fix: refuse local tsgo under host pressure
target.body:
## Summary
- refuse local native type-aware checks by default on developer/agent hosts
- cover `tsgo`, type-aware `oxlint`, and sharded type-aware `oxlint`
- cover direct raw `tsgo` helper scripts: extension package boundary, core boundary, and tsgo profiling
- keep metadata commands cheap and unblocked (`--help`, `--version`, etc.)
- keep CI/GitHub Actions unblocked so broad native checks run online instead of on constrained hosts
- preserve explicit break-glass overrides for local runs
- keep pressure refusal and effective heavy-check temp-dir checks from the earlier patch
- rebase onto current `main` and preserve upstream serial shard support

## Why
A constrained Raspberry Pi/agent host repeatedly became unstable when native type-aware checks (`tsgo`/`tsgolint` via type-aware oxlint) ran locally. Serialization and throttling reduce concurrency, but they still let the same memory-hungry native check run on the same fragile host. That is the wrong failure mode.

This PR now makes the safer default explicit: local wrappers and repo helper scripts refuse native type-aware checks unless the operator opts in. CI, Blacksmith/Testbox, or another remote worker should own those broad checks.

## Behavior
Local native type-aware checks refuse by default when local heavy-check policy is active and mode is not `full`:

- `scripts/run-tsgo.mjs`
- `scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` when it runs type-aware oxlint, including explicit file-target runs
- `scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs` before artifact prep or child shard spawning
- `scripts/check-extension-package-tsc-boundary.mjs`
- `scripts/check-tsgo-core-boundary.mjs`
- `scripts/profile-tsgo.mjs`

Bypasses / allowed cases:

- GitHub Actions / CI are not blocked
- metadata-only commands are not blocked
- `OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=full` allows the local run
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_FORCE=1` allows the local run
- `OPENCLAW_ALLOW_LOCAL_NATIVE_TYPECHECK=1` allows the local run
- child processes already covered by a parent heavy-check lock can continue without self-deadlock

Existing pressure checks still refuse local heavy checks when headroom is too low:

- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_MIN_MEM_AVAILABLE_BYTES` defaults to `1.5 GiB`
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_MIN_TMP_AVAILABLE_BYTES` defaults to `1 GiB`
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_MAX_TMP_USED_PERCENT` defaults to `80`

## GitHub CI note
GitHub CI means these checks run online on GitHub/runner machines after pushing the branch, not on the local Raspberry Pi. The PR gets pass/fail status from those remote runners.

## Test Plan
Final head: `21425d2a82eba1f6900f7c58a9129ba234a2ef68`

Local checks intentionally stayed narrow and refusal-only. Broad lint/test/type-aware gates are left to GitHub CI.

Narrow local syntax/refusal probes run after the latest patch:

- `node --check scripts/check-extension-package-tsc-boundary.mjs`
- `node --check scripts/profile-tsgo.mjs`
- `node --check scripts/check-tsgo-core-boundary.mjs`
- `OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled node scripts/check-extension-package-tsc-boundary.mjs --mode=compile`
- `OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled node scripts/profile-tsgo.mjs core --json`
- `OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled node scripts/check-tsgo-core-boundary.mjs`

Previously passed locally before this direct-script follow-up:

- syntax checks for `run-tsgo`, `run-oxlint`, `run-oxlint-shards`, and local heavy-check runtime
- scoped formatter/lint checks on touched wrapper files
- `OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled pnpm test test/scripts/local-heavy-check-runtime.test.ts test/scripts/run-oxlint.test.ts --run`
- result: `Test Files 2 passed`, `Tests 34 passed`

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Local OpenClaw script entry points that can start native `tsgo`/`tsgolint` now refuse on this Raspberry Pi/agent host by default, so accidental local type-aware checks are pushed to GitHub CI/Blacksmith/Testbox instead of consuming local memory.
- **Real environment tested:** Raspberry Pi OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/local-tsgo-pressure-guard`, head `21425d2a82eba1f6900f7c58a9129ba234a2ef68`, with `OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
node --check scripts/check-extension-package-tsc-boundary.mjs
node --check scripts/profile-tsgo.mjs
node --check scripts/check-tsgo-core-boundary.mjs
OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled node scripts/check-extension-package-tsc-boundary.mjs --mode=compile
OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled node scripts/profile-tsgo.mjs core --json
OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled node scripts/check-tsgo-core-boundary.mjs
pgrep -a -f 'tsgolint|typescript/native-preview/bin/tsgo|node_modules/.bin/tsgo' || true
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output from the real Raspberry Pi checkout:

```text
syntax ok
boundary=1 profile=1 core=1
Refusing to start extension package boundary tsgo on this local host.
Native type-aware checks can exhaust constrained developer/agent machines; run them in GitHub CI, Blacksmith/Testbox, or another remote worker instead.
[tsgo-profile] profiling core
Refusing to start tsgo profile on this local host.
Native type-aware checks can exhaust constrained developer/agent machines; run them in GitHub CI, Blacksmith/Testbox, or another remote worker instead.
Refusing to start core boundary tsgo on this local host.
Native type-aware checks can exhaust constrained developer/agent machines; run them in GitHub CI, Blacksmith/Testbox, or another remote worker instead.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The direct local `tsgo` helper scripts exited non-zero with refusal messages before spawning `tsgo`/`tsgolint`; the process check found no real native `tsgo`/`tsgolint` worker running.
- **What was not tested:** Broad local CI was intentionally not run on the Raspberry Pi. Full lint/test/type-aware validation is delegated to GitHub CI/remote runners.

target.title: feat(anthropic): claude-cli-interactive backend — stream reasoning via local TLS proxy (experimental)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem.** OpenClaw's `claude-cli` backend invokes `claude -p --output-format stream-json` (headless). Starting **June 15, 2026** that path moves onto Anthropic's separate programmatic credit pool ([VentureBeat coverage](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-reinstates-openclaw-and-third-party-agent-usage-on-claude-subscriptions-with-a-catch)) — fixed monthly allowance, billed at API rates, doesn't roll over.
- **Why it matters.** Every OpenClaw Opus turn on a Claude subscription will drain that fixed credit post–June 15 instead of counting against the interactive subscription limits subscribers are paying for. Opus turns exhaust the credit fast.
- **What changed.** New opt-in `claude-cli-interactive` backend. Each turn spawns one fresh `bun wrapper.ts …` that boots a loopback HTTPS MITM on a 127.0.0.1 port, taps every API SSE event, re-emits the events as `claude -p --output-format stream-json` JSONL records, and kills the claude child on `end_turn`. A `tty-spoof.cjs` `NODE_OPTIONS=--require` preload keeps claude in interactive mode under piped stdio.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary).** Existing `claude-cli` backend untouched. `agents.defaults.cliBackends.claude-cli` user overrides inherit into the interactive backend via `inheritUserConfigFrom` with `-p`-mode flags stripped. System CA store is not mutated (`NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` scoped to the spawned claude child only). Non–`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels pass through unmodified. Tool-use rendering / waiting-on-tool injection lands in #82285, not here.
- **AI-assisted:** yes — drafted with Claude.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `claude` running under OpenClaw in interactive (subscription) mode with full `init` / `stream_event*` / `result` JSONL reconstruction on the wrapper's stdout, sourced from the loopback MITM proxy's SSE tap.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows 11 Pro 26200, Node v24.12.0, pnpm, Bun, `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` v2.1.142 (npm-installed), Opus 4.7 (`claude-opus-4-7`), live OpenClaw gateway driving the backend per-turn over Telegram.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `unset NODE_OPTIONS && pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic/cli-backend-interactive.test.ts`; `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/anthropic/tsconfig.json`; set `cliBackends.claude-cli-interactive` active in user config; restart the OpenClaw gateway; send a Telegram message that triggers an Opus 4.7 turn; observe wrapper boot in stderr, SSE flow through `127.0.0.1:<port>`, JSONL arriving at OpenClaw; send a follow-up message with `--resume <sessionId>` to verify resume.
- **Evidence after fix:**
  - vitest: `Test Files 1 passed (1) / Tests 5 passed (5)` — `normalizeConfig` branches (inherited claude-cli match, direct Bun-path override, env passthrough, no-override skip).
  - Gateway log excerpt + cert listing + active-config snapshot on the [`proof-assets/pr-81851`](https://github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/tree/proof-assets/pr-81851/.proof) branch. Embedded sample (one of three real turns in the gateway-log excerpt):

    ```
    cli exec: provider=claude-cli-interactive model=opus useResume=true session=present
    cli argv: bun <NPM>\…\wrapper.ts --effort max --verbose --setting-sources user --resume <SESSION> --allowedTools mcp__openclaw__* --model opus <prompt:N chars>
    [openclaw-proxy] certs ready: <HOME>\.openclaw\proxy-certs\ca.crt
    [openclaw-proxy] MITM proxy active on 127.0.0.1:17081 — Anthropic API traffic intercepted locally to capture thinking_delta events
    [openclaw-proxy] spawning claude binary: <NPM>\…\@anthropic-ai\claude-code\bin\claude.exe
    [openclaw-proxy] result flushed, waiting for session write before kill
    [openclaw-proxy] session file updated, killing claude
    ```
- **Observed result after fix:** per-turn lifecycle terminates cleanly — wrapper emits `init`, a continuous stream of `stream_event` records, then a single `result` on `end_turn`, sets `intentionalKill = true`, and `claude.kill()`s the child so the per-turn process tree exits 0.
- **What was not tested:** Linux/macOS (developed on Windows 11 only); bundled-MCP `claude-config-file` mode end-to-end with multiple MCP servers active (only `mcp__openclaw__*` exercised live); forced Anthropic-side `429` / `overloaded_error` mid-stream (the `api_error` synthetic event path is implemented but I did not provoke a real 429).

## Change Type

- [x] Feature

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Integrations

## Linked

- Related: #81550 (programmatic-mode reasoning bridge — this PR is the interactive-mode equivalent on a separate backend); #82285 (tool-use injection, builds on the `stream_event` surface this PR establishes).

## Root Cause

N/A — feature PR. The lack of thinking visibility on subscription is a property of `claude -p` (headless) mode, not a regression in OpenClaw.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target:** `extensions/anthropic/cli-backend-interactive.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** `normalizeConfig` post-merge shape across four branches — inherited claude-cli match (forces `command="bun"`, stashes binary on env), direct Bun-path override (keeps the override as the spawn command, no env stash), env passthrough preservation, no-override case (env passthrough omitted so wrapper falls back to PATH lookup of `"claude"`).
- **Why smallest reliable guardrail:** the failure mode is config-shape drift inside `normalizeConfig` — fully exercised synchronously without spawning bun or claude, runs in ms vs the minutes an e2e run would cost.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** newly-installed user with no inherited `claude-cli` override could spawn `bun` as the claude binary if `normalizeConfig` stashed `merged.command` indiscriminately.
  - **Mitigation:** `normalizeConfig` consults `context.config.agents.defaults.cliBackends.claude-cli.command`; only stashes the env passthrough on a match. Regression test asserts the env var is `undefined` for the no-override branch.
- **Risk:** `tty-spoof.cjs` is a runtime support file (not a build output) and could be omitted from packaged installs.
  - **Mitigation:** declared in `extensions/anthropic/package.json#openclaw.build.staticAssets`; postbuild asset copier ships it alongside the wrapper.
- **Risk:** winget-installed `claude.exe` shadows the npm install via PATHEXT on Windows.
  - **Mitigation:** wrapper spawns `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_CLAUDE_BINARY` directly when the parent backend resolved a binary, never falling back to a bare PATH lookup.
- **Risk:** title-gen / Haiku side-requests inject `{"title":"…"}` artifacts into the reasoning stream.
  - **Mitigation:** mitm-server classifies each `/v1/messages` request into `auxiliary` (no tools) / `compaction` (compact.ts prompt markers) / `tool_followup` (last msg has tool_result) / `normal`. Wrapper drops auxiliary entirely, streams compaction as `thinking_delta`, emits a result only on `normal` `end_turn`. Response-content fingerprint backup (`<analysis>` + Primary-Request-and-Intent + Pending-Tasks) catches anything that slipped past the request-level classifier.
- **Risk:** spilled-prompt overflow file leaks to concurrent runs via shared tempdir + `--add-dir`.
  - **Mitigation:** per-run subdir under `${tmpdir()}/openclaw-interactive-proxy/run-<32-hex-bytes>/` (0700), file 0600, `--add-dir` scoped to the per-run subdir only.
- **Risk:** local CA + leaf key persisted under user's OpenClaw config dir could be reused if an attacker gets read access.
  - **Mitigation:** trust scoped to the spawned claude child via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` only (no system trust mutation); CA issues solely the one `api.anthropic.com` leaf the per-turn proxy needs; key files hardened to 0600 with fail-closed regeneration; maintainer security review invited.
- **Risk:** Anthropic-side non-SSE 4xx/5xx on `/v1/messages` (429, billing, overloaded) hangs the wrapper because claude doesn't self-exit on API error.
  - **Mitigation:** mitm-server emits a synthetic `api_error` event for non-SSE 4xx/5xx; wrapper surfaces a `result` with `is_error: true` and exits nonzero so the CLI runner treats the turn as failed and triggers failover.
- **Risk:** two resumes of the same Claude session race on the same transcript.
  - **Mitigation:** backend config sets `serialize: true` so the framework queues same-session runs; different sessions still run concurrently. mitm-server binds both proxy stages on port 0 so concurrent proxies can't collide on a preferred port either.

## Security Impact

- **New permissions/capabilities:** Yes — per-turn local TLS MITM on a loopback port. Scoped to the spawned claude child via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS`; system trust store untouched. Local CA + leaf key cached under user's OpenClaw config dir at 0700/0600.
- **Secrets/tokens handling changed:** No — wrapper does not read, log, or modify the Anthropic auth token; it is forwarded byte-for-byte to the re-issued upstream request.
- **New/changed network calls:** Yes — claude's outbound HTTPS to `api.anthropic.com:443` is rerouted via `HTTPS_PROXY=http://127.0.0.1:<port>` inside the child. Non–`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels (statsig, datadog, etc.) bypass the TLS terminator entirely.
- **Command/tool execution surface changed:** No — same `claude` binary, same allowed-tools (`mcp__openclaw__*`), same session semantics.
- **Data access scope changed:** No — proxy sees only the SSE bytes claude itself sees on the wire; nothing exfiltrated or aggregated outside the per-turn process.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible: existing `claude-cli` backend unchanged. Opt-in via the `claude-cli-interactive` id; no default flips.
- New optional env: `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_PROXY_DEBUG=1` enables verbose wrapper stderr.
- Migration needed: no.

## Prior art

[`smithersai/claude-p`](https://github.com/smithersai/claude-p) runs `claude` without `-p` but bypasses the JSONL streaming surface entirely — works for the raw final text but loses the `stream_event` records that reasoning and tool-use rendering depend on. This PR reproduces the `-p --output-format stream-json` shape record-for-record from the SSE tap so existing OpenClaw stream consumers layer on unchanged.

target.title: feat(diagnostics): add skill usage telemetry
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: tool execution telemetry existed, but skill usage was not exported as a first-class diagnostics signal.
- Why it matters: operators could see `read`/`exec` tool calls, but could not build Prometheus/Grafana or OTEL dashboards showing which skills were actually used.
- What changed: added trusted `skill.used` diagnostics when a run reads a known `SKILL.md` or completes a command-dispatched skill tool, then exported it via `diagnostics-otel` and `diagnostics-prometheus`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no skill loading, skill selection, command execution, permissions, auth, or model behavior changed.

## Change Type (select all)

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: skill usage is now visible as diagnostics telemetry instead of only indirect tool calls.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw repo on macOS, Node 22.22.2.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts extensions/diagnostics-prometheus/src/service.test.ts extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: targeted tests passed: 3 Vitest shards, 3 files, 91 tests total.
- Observed result after fix: core emits `skill.used`; Prometheus renders `openclaw_skill_used_total`; OTEL records `openclaw.skill.used` counter/span.
- What was not tested: live Gateway + Grafana dashboard scrape.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: N/A.
- Missing detection / guardrail: N/A.
- Contributing context (if known): tool telemetry counted tool calls, but skills are prompt/read based and did not previously have their own diagnostic event.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A for bug regression.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] End-to-end test

- Target test or file: `src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts`, `extensions/diagnostics-prometheus/src/service.test.ts`, `extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.test.ts`

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

No config changes required.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
agent reads SKILL.md -> read tool telemetry only -> no skill usage dashboard signal

After:
agent reads SKILL.md -> skill.used diagnostic -> OTEL/Prometheus export -> dashboardable skill usage
